reasoning: The FRED data reveals that commercial bank auto loan totals have been in a broad decline over the past year (dropping from ~495B in March 2024 to ~485B in March 2025). The series hit a trough in August 2024 (~484.6B) and recovered slightly into January 2025 (~487.4B) before falling again steadily throughout early 2025 to 485.5B. Given the recent string of downward weeks (with only a small +0.13 bounce in the latest week), near-term momentum is negative. Therefore, short horizons lean toward a continued decrease (p=0.42 to 0.45). Given the potential for interest rate stabilization or cuts later in 2025, the medium-to-long term is less certain, with probabilities reverting closely to 0.50 as the timeline extends to early 2026.
